title: Body Worn Camera Policies

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am writing to request all department policies, directives, and procedures that have governed the department's use of body-worn cameras (BWCs) since 2020. In particular, I am requesting policies that mandate release of footage to the public or outline the procedure for public release outside of public records law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jeffrey Lamson

communications.subject: Confirmation of Public Records Request
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting the Florida Department of Law Enforcement Office of Open Government. This message is being provided as confirmation your message was received. You should receive a response to your inquiry as soon as possible.



If you need criminal history records, please visit our website, https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/Criminal-History-Records/Obtaining-Criminal-History-Information.aspx, and/or contact FDLE’s Criminal History Services Section at ApplicantChecks@fdle.state.fl.us<mailto:ApplicantChecks@fdle.state.fl.us> or (850) 410-8161.



Public records requests are processed in the order that they are received. Processing time may be lengthened due to the nature and extent of the request. Searches may be negative and FDLE may not have records responsive to your public records request. You may also receive an invoice for your request depending on the nature and extent of your public records request. Please feel free to contact us at (850) 410-7676, or publicrecords@fdle.state.fl.us<mailto:publicrecords@fdle.state.fl.us>, if you have additional specific information relating to the records you are requesting, as this may help expedite your request and/or reduce costs that may be associated with the request.



Additional information regarding how to obtain public records including what costs may be applicable is contained from the hyperlink below. We are committed to our fundamental values of Service, Integrity, Respect, and Quality.



For Additional Information click here: Open Government (state.fl.us)<https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/Open-Government>

title: Flock Event Logs

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Flock Event Logs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All audit logs, access logs, or activity records generated by or within the Flock Safety platform reflecting any activity by Lawrenceville Police Department personnel, from June 4, 2025 through the date this request is processed.
2. All records reflecting the granting, approval, configuration, or receipt of access to any Flock Safety camera network shared by another agency with Lawrenceville Police Department, including but not limited to any communications, system notifications, or authorization records related to any such network share.
3. Any written policies, procedures, or guidance governing Lawrenceville Police Department personnel's use of camera feeds received via the Flock Safety network sharing system, including any restrictions on accessing networks shared by other agencies.
For purposes of this request, "Flock Safety platform" includes any Flock Safety product, application, dashboard, or interface, including but not limited to Flock OS, Flock's law enforcement portal, and any associated livestream or video access tools.
I request that responsive records be provided in electronic format. If any portion of this request is denied, please provide a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon for each withheld record.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

title: Flock Nova Platform Audit Logs

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Flock Nova Platform Audit Logs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Flock Nova Audit Logs
All audit logs, access logs, event logs, and activity records generated within the Flock Safety platform reflecting use of, or access to, the Nova feature by any user associated with the Dunwoody PD organization, for the period June 30, 2025 through the date of this request.
For context, Dunwoody PD's Nova feature was enabled on or about June 30, 2025 (Flock audit event ID: 869ade3e-85d4-50b2-b426-74cae52b32eb), as reflected in existing audit records already in the city's possession.
Specifically, I request records sufficient to identify:
a. All Nova searches, queries, or lookups conducted by any Dunwoody PD user or any Flock Safety employee operating under Dunwoody credentials, including the initiating user, timestamp, search parameters, and any associated case number or stated purpose;
b. All records reflecting the results returned by Nova queries, to the extent retained by the city;
c. Any policies, procedures, training materials, or usage guidelines provided to Dunwoody PD in connection with Nova access.
2. Responsive Format
I request that records be produced in their native digital format (CSV, XLSX, JSON, or equivalent) where available, rather than printed or converted to PDF. If records exist only in a non-exportable format, please describe the format and available export options before producing.
If any costs are anticipated, please provide an itemized cost estimate — broken down by staff time, hourly rate, and any per-page or processing fees — prior to beginning production, as required by O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

title: Public Data Request – September 30, 2023 Fatal Crash (Irma & Wilma Miller) – Fillmore County (Fillmore County Sheriff's Office Preston, Minnesota, Usa)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Dear Custodian of Records,

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act (Minn. Stat. Ch. 13), I respectfully request access to the following multimedia records related to a fatal motor vehicle collision involving an Amish buggy and a Toyota 4Runner that occurred on September 30, 2023, at approximately 8:25 AM – 9:30 AM at County Road 1 near County Road 102, Sumner Township, Fillmore County, Minnesota, resulting in the deaths of Irma Miller and Wilma Miller.

Example Requested Records:

911 Audio
911 audio of the call(s) placed by passersby or involved parties on September 30, 2023, at approximately 8:25 AM – 8:45 AM, reporting a motor vehicle collision involving an Amish buggy at County Road 1 near County Road 102, Fillmore County, Minnesota. (Caller name unknown)
Body-Worn Camera – Initial Response
Body-worn camera video and audio from the first responding officer(s) at County Road 1 near County Road 102, documenting arrival, initial scene assessment, and confirmation of deceased victim(s), including Irma Miller and Wilma Miller.

Body-Worn Camera – Specific Event (Witness Statement)
Body-worn camera video and audio capturing the initial statement provided by Sarah Peterson at approximately 8:30 AM – 9:30 AM on September 30, 2023, at the crash scene, including the identification of herself as the driver of the Toyota 4Runner.

In-Car Dash Camera
In-car dash camera video and audio capturing the arrival, positioning, and on-scene activity of Fillmore County Sheriff’s Office vehicles at approximately 8:25 AM on September 30, 2023, at County Road 1 near County Road 102, including the recorded interactions at the scene.

Body-Worn Camera – Evidence Collection
Body-worn camera video and audio documenting evidence collection at the scene on September 30, 2023, including documentation of the Amish buggy wreckage, Toyota 4Runner damage, roadway debris, and the recovered items or suspected contraband.

Crime Scene Photographs
Crime scene photographs taken at County Road 1 and County Road 102 depict the impact point, final resting positions of the Amish buggy and Toyota 4Runner, and the deceased victims as documented at the scene.

Evidence Processing Photographs
Evidence processing photographs associated with Case Nos. 23-CR-24-106 and 23-CR-24-107, specifically including:
Damage to the Toyota 4Runner
The destroyed Amish buggy
the digital evidence images, such as screenshots or photographs of text messages recovered from suspect mobile devices

Body-Worn Camera / Dash Camera – Arrest or Post-Investigation Contact
Body-worn camera/ dashcam/MVR video and audio documenting the arrest, charging contact, or surrender/processing of Samantha Jo Peterson and Sarah Beth Peterson following the investigation (approximately February 2024), including the custody transfer or booking-related footage if available.

Interrogation Room Recordings
Full interview room video and audio recording(s) of the custodial or non-custodial interviews of Samantha Jo Peterson and Sarah Beth Peterson conducted by the Fillmore County Sheriff’s Office or the Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension between September 30, 2023, and February 2024.

Witness Interviews – Rochester Hy-Vee Employees
Full video and audio recording(s) of witness interviews conducted with coworkers at the Rochester Hy-Vee by the Fillmore County Sheriff’s Office following the incident, specifically regarding the statements made by the suspects about the crash or related events.

CCTV / surveillance video from Hy-Vee (Rochester, MN) for the period 7:00 AM – 10:00 AM on September 30, 2023, depicting the Peterson twins' arrival/departure or the interactions with coworkers immediately following the crash.

Incident Details:

Incident Numbers: 23-CR-24-106 and 23-CR-24-107
Victim(s): Irma Miller, Wilma Miller
Subject(s): Sarah Peterson, Samantha Jo Peterson, Sarah Beth Peterson 
Date of Incident: September 30, 2023
Time of Incident: Approximately 8:25 AM – 9:30 AM
Location of Incident: County Road 1 near County Road 102, Sumner Township, Fillmore County, Minnesota

This request seeks native digital copies of the above-listed multimedia records as maintained by your agency. I am not requesting written reports, CAD logs, transcripts, or administrative summaries at this time.

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption for each denial and release all reasonably segregable portions of responsive records.

Please provide an itemized cost estimate prior to processing. I prefer electronic delivery via secure download link or USB drive.

If your agency does not maintain any portion of these records, please forward this request to the appropriate custodian or advise me accordingly.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Danielle D.

title: Payments to Jessie Liang (San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

To the Custodian of Records, San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency:
Pursuant to the California Public Records Act (Gov. Code § 7920 et seq.), I hereby request copies of the following records:
	1.	All payments, disbursements, contracts, invoices, purchase orders, or financial transactions made by the San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency (SFMTA) to Jesse Liang, whether as an individual, sole proprietor, or through any business entity associated with Jesse Liang.
	2.	All records sufficient to identify the amount, date, purpose, and authorization of each such payment, including but not limited to:
a. Warrants or checks issued
b. Electronic fund transfers (ACH/wire)
c. Contract documents or agreements
d. Vendor registration records
e. W-9 forms or 1099 records (redacted to the extent required by law)
This request covers all responsive records from January 1, 2015 through the date this request is processed, or the full extent of SFMTA’s records, whichever is greater.
I request that any portions exempt from disclosure be redacted rather than withheld in their entirety, with the specific exemption cited for each redaction.
Please provide records in electronic format (PDF or comparable) where available. If any portion of this request is unclear, I ask that you contact me before making any adverse determination so that I may clarify.
Pursuant to Gov. Code § 7922.530, please respond within 10 days confirming whether responsive records exist and will be produced.
Thank you for your attention to this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ajay Aggarwal

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all complaints made to the New York Attorney General's office about or regarding any of the following hospitals, medical centers, or clinics or doctors practicing at any these facilities, for the dates March 1, 2024 to February 28, 2026:
1. Hospital for Special Surgery (HSS)
2. Mount Sinai
3. Nuvance Health
4. Vassar Brothers Medical Center
5. NYU Langone

In these records we would like to see the following information, if available:

1. The text of the complaint, else a summary if text is not available.
2. Date complaint was filed
3. Current status of complaint / whether resolved
4. Amount in dispute, if regarding financial complaint
5. Date of service with the provider
6. Date of payment made to the provider, if available.
7. Any further information, if available.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

communications.subject: Indiana Access to Public Records Act Request: Flock Email Requests (Indiana University Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Indiana Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide all records of email communications, including attachments and calendar invitations, that were sent to or received from the domain “@flocksafety.com,” between November 1, 2025 and the date this request is fulfilled.

This request includes:
- Emails where @flocksafety.com appears in the sender, recipient (To, CC, BCC), or within the body of the message
- Attachments of any file type, including but not limited to PDFs, presentations, spreadsheets, and images
- Calendar invitations, meeting requests, and related responses involving Flock Safety personnel

Please search all agency-controlled email systems and accounts reasonably likely to contain responsive records, including individual officer accounts, group mailboxes, and archived or backup systems. Records should be provided in native electronic format where possible (e.g., .pst, .msg, or .eml with metadata preserved), including all available metadata fields such as sender, recipients, timestamps, and subject lines.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

This is to acknowledge receipt of your April 21, 2026, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request for "the following records:Any and all materials related to language, regional expertise, and culture (LREC) education and training given to those stationed in Israel, current as of April 2026."(Date Range for Record Search: From 01/01/2015 To 04/21/2026).  We have assigned number 2026-04432-F to identify your request. 
Our agency is committed to processing your request in a timely and complete manner. However, we have determined that your request, as currently written, is too broad in scope to allow for a meaningful search for records. The FOIA requires that a request must "reasonably describe" the records sought (5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(3)(A)). Requests that are vague or seek 'any and all documents,' 'any documents,' or 'all documents' are considered impermissibly broad and do not meet this statutory requirement to 'reasonably describe such records', as they would require an unreasonably burdensome search. (See Exxon Mobil Corp. v. U.S. Dep't of Interior ). 
To proceed, we require your assistance in narrowing the scope of your request. A focused request enables us to conduct an efficient, targeted search and provide you with relevant records more quickly. To help us process your request, please provide more detail on the following: 

 Department of the Air Force (DAF) Involvement: Your request currently does not sufficiently identify any related DAF mission, policy or action. Please describe the specific subject, program, or event related to the DAF that your request concerns. Providing context, such as the date and circumstances of an event or the purpose of a program, allows us to conduct an organized, non-random search 
 Record Locations: Please identify the specific agency offices, directorates, or personnel you believe would be the originators or custodians of the requested records. 
 Specific Document Types: Please advise what specific Air Force documents you are seeking (Generally, a record is reasonably described when the description contains sufficient file-related information (type of document, title, subject area, date of creation, originator, etc.); or the request contains enough event-related information (date and circumstances surrounding the event the record covers) to permit the conduct of an organized, non-random search.) 
 Specific Time Frame: Please provide a specific date range for the records you are seeking (e.g., January 1, 2020, to December 31, 2023). A request without a time frame or a request seeking decades long periods is generally considered overly broad. 
Please note that the FOIA does not require agencies to answer questions or interrogatories; analyze and/or interpret documents for a requester; create records; conduct research; initiate investigations; or provide statutes, regulations, publications, or other documents that are otherwise made available to the public.  Requests only apply to records in existence at the time your request is received. 
Providing us with these details is crucial as it permits us to conduct an efficient search, allowing our staff to conduct a focused search within the appropriate databases and systems.  It provided for efficient resource allocation, which helps us dedicate our resources effectively to locating records that directly correspond to your request.  Finally, it allows us to provide a more timely response. 
If we do not receive the requested clarification by 2 June 2026, your request will be administratively closed. If you are dissatisfied with the servicing of this request or in this response from this office, you may contact the Air Force FOIA Public Liaison Officer, Mr. Robert Bivins, concerning this response at AF FOIA Public Liaison Office (SAF/CNZA), 1800 Air Force Pentagon, Washington, DC 20330-1800, DAF.FOIA@US.AF.MIL or (703) 614-8500. Additionally, you may contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) at the National Archives and Records Administration to inquire about the FOIA mediation services they offer. The contact information for OGIS is as follows: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at OGIS@NARA.GOV; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769. 
We appreciate your understanding and cooperation in this matter. Please direct your questions to SAF.AA.HAF.FOIA.WORKFLOW@US.AF.MIL and use this assigned number, 2026-04432-F, to assist us in responding more promptly. Very Respectfully, SAF/AAGI FOIA TEAM Government Information Management Branch (SAF/AAGI) Office of the Secretary of the Air Force 1000 Air Force Pentagon Washington, DC 20330-1000

title: LREC Materials for Israel (II) (United States Department of the Army)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: LREC Materials for Israel (II) (United States Department of the Army)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all materials related to language, regional expertise, and culture (LREC) education and training given to those stationed in Israel, current as of April 2026. Please include handouts, brochures, pamphlets, Power Points, directives, memorandums, lecture notes, or any other relevant material.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an assistant editor at Reason Magazine, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti
